title: Informative explainer video: brand-graded sizzle cut, cleaned VO, and on-brand lower-third / call-out graphic plates
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com brief: raw footage shaped into a clear informative video; on-brand text overlays (lower-thirds, call-outs) to reinforce facts/figures; simple polished graphics / light motion to underline transitions; the freelancer handles trimming/sequencing, color correction, audio balancing, exporting; client supplies footage, logo assets, color references, and a loose narrative outline; deliverables = final video in 1080p (and 4K if practical) H.264, all custom text overlays/motion graphics rendered in the cut, and project/linked assets organised for easy future tweaks. REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark, and tagged honestly: (a) the brief is open-ended ("raw footage") — I fix it to one ~3-min talking-head + b-roll bundle plus a separate VO scratch track so the [A] async video/audio tools have concrete inputs; (b) "color correction" of the *footage* is the brief's intent, but the headless connector cannot grade a moving video frame-by-frame, so the color work is honestly split: the editor's master grade lives in the [A] reframe/cut deliverables, while the connector does the *reference/plate* color pipeline — pulling the client's color-reference still through the full Lightroom-class adjust_* chain to LOCK the brand LUT/look that the editor then matches in Premiere, plus grading the end-card hero frame; (c) "motion graphics / text overlays" — the connector does NOT composite text onto video (out of scope), so the lower-third bar, call-out badge, vectorized logo, and end-card are produced as standalone on-brand graphic PLATES (transparent PNG / SVG) that the editor drops onto the timeline — this is exactly the brief's "linked assets, organised for easy future tweaks". No gen-fill, no AI object removal, no text-to-image, no compositing, no video trim-to-timestamp, no format-convert is claimed; image_fill_area is solid-color only; video_resize keeps the same length; media_enhance_speech is voice-only. Every video/audio step is tagged [A] (async-widget) and every still/plate step [C].
one_line_ask: Turn my raw informative-explainer footage, scratch voiceover, brand logo, and color-reference still into a polished short cut: clean the VO, build a brand-locked color look plus a graded end-card, and produce on-brand lower-third, call-out, and vectorized-logo graphic plates — then deliver a 1080p sizzle and a vertical reframe, all assets organised on a board for the Premiere project.
full_brief:
I have raw footage for an informative explainer video that needs to be shaped into a clear, engaging cut, plus the supporting brand assets to dress it. Source material I'm supplying: (1) a bundle of raw clips — a ~3-minute talking-head segment intercut with product/process b-roll; (2) a separate scratch voiceover WAV recorded in a noisy room that needs cleaning before it can carry the edit; (3) my company logo as a flat PNG; (4) a single "color reference" photograph that represents the warm, slightly cinematic brand look I want the whole piece graded toward; and (5) a loose narrative outline.

I need the freelancer to handle the full edit and the supporting graphics, mapped to Adobe Creative Cloud connector tools, with every step honestly tagged for execution mode:

A. AUDIO — clean my scratch voiceover so it can drive the cut: denoise / de-room the voice track (voice-only; this is not music mastering).

B. VIDEO — (1) summarize the raw footage so we agree on the key talking points and a logical flow before cutting; (2) produce a punchy ~60-second highlight/sizzle cut from the raw clips that surfaces those key points; (3) reframe that cut to a 9:16 vertical version for social teasers, same length (no trimming).

C. BRAND COLOR LOOK — from my color-reference still, build the locked "brand LUT" look the editor will match the master grade to: straighten and crop it clean, then run the full Lightroom-class grade (auto-tone baseline, exposure, highlights, dark-portion lift, warm color-temperature push, HSL + vibrance/saturation shaping, a gentle preset and fine grain) so I have one authoritative graded reference frame.

D. GRAPHIC PLATES — the on-brand overlays the editor lays onto the timeline (delivered as standalone transparent PNG / SVG plates, NOT composited onto the video): (1) vectorize my logo to a clean SVG so it scales for the end-card and corner bug; (2) build a lower-third bar plate — solid brand-color bar generated by selecting a region and filling it; (3) build a call-out badge plate the same way; (4) grade the end-card hero frame to match the brand look.

E. PACKAGING — gather the graded reference, the plates, the cleaned VO, and the video deliverables onto a Firefly board so the whole linked-asset set is organised for easy future tweaks, exactly as I want the Premiere project handed off.

Deliverables on approval: cleaned voiceover; a ~60s 1080p H.264 sizzle cut and a 9:16 reframe (same length); one authoritative graded brand-reference frame; logo SVG; lower-third and call-out PNG plates; a graded end-card frame; and a Firefly board linking the whole organised asset set. Two rounds of feedback should be plenty.

inputs.name: raw_explainer_clips_bundle
inputs.gen_prompt: 1080p documentary-style raw footage bundle for a corporate informative explainer: a mid-30s presenter in a modern office speaking to camera in a medium shot (natural window light, slight handheld motion, real skin pores and stray hair flyaways, candid not glossy), intercut with b-roll of hands using a laptop dashboard, a close-up of a circuit/product detail, and a wide of a small team at a desk. Neutral ungraded color, slightly flat contrast as if straight off the camera, native ambient audio. ~3 minutes of usable takes.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look ungraded and genuinely raw (flat contrast, neutral WB, minor handheld wobble) so the sizzle-cut, reframe, and brand-grade steps have something real to improve. Documentary realism, not CGI-clean.

inputs.name: scratch_voiceover.wav
inputs.gen_prompt: A warm, conversational male narrator reading ~45s of explainer voiceover script: 'Every day your team loses hours to scattered tools. Here's how one connected dashboard changes that...' Capture as WAV, then deliberately degrade for an audio-repair task: add room reverb, low HVAC hum, and faint background chatter so it sounds recorded in a noisy office.
inputs.realism_notes: Intentionally noisy/roomy (NOT clean 48k loudnorm) because this is the input to media_enhance_speech — the repair must have something to fix. Voice only, no music bed.

inputs.name: company_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, clean vector-style corporate logo on a transparent background: a stylized geometric hexagon mark beside the wordmark 'NORTHLOOP' in a modern geometric sans-serif, single warm-amber brand color (#E8A13A) with one deep-navy accent. Crisp edges, no gradients, no photographic texture — suitable for vectorizing.
inputs.realism_notes: Flat solid-color logo with crisp edges so image_vectorize produces a clean SVG. Text/logo asset => gemini-3-pro-image.

inputs.name: brand_color_reference.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A warm, slightly cinematic interior photograph used as a brand color reference: golden-hour light raking across a wooden desk with a laptop and coffee cup, amber and teal undertones, soft film-like contrast, gentle grain, realistic depth of field. Shot slightly crooked/handheld with a small horizon tilt, framed loosely with extra headroom (so it needs straightening and cropping).
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately tilted and loosely framed so auto_straighten + crop_to_bounds have real work; warm cinematic tones give the adjust_* grade chain a believable target. Photoreal => gpt-image-2.

inputs.name: endcard_hero_frame.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean medium-wide hero still suitable for a video end-card: the same presenter and modern office from the footage, smiling toward camera with negative space on the right for a logo lockup, neutral ungraded color and flat contrast straight off the camera. Photoreal, documentary lighting, real skin texture.
inputs.realism_notes: Ungraded/flat so the end-card grade step visibly matches it to the locked brand look; negative space on the right leaves room for the vectorized logo plate. Photoreal => gpt-image-2.

outputs.name: cleaned_voiceover.wav
outputs.spec: Denoised / de-reverbed voiceover track, voice-only, suitable as the edit's narration bed (media_enhance_speech output).

outputs.name: sizzle_cut_1080p.mp4
outputs.spec: ~60s AI highlight/sizzle cut of the raw clips surfacing the key talking points, 1920x1080 (H.264 target on export).

outputs.name: sizzle_cut_vertical_9x16.mp4
outputs.spec: 9:16 (1080x1920) reframe of the sizzle cut, SAME length (no trimming), for social teasers.

outputs.name: brand_look_reference_frame.jpg
outputs.spec: One authoritative graded brand-reference frame: straightened, cropped, and run through the full Lightroom-class grade — the locked look the editor matches the master grade to.

outputs.name: logo_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized SVG of the company logo for scalable end-card and corner-bug use.

outputs.name: lower_third_bar_plate.png
outputs.spec: Transparent-background lower-third bar plate — solid brand-amber bar region filled via selection-fill, ready to drop on the Premiere timeline.

outputs.name: callout_badge_plate.png
outputs.spec: Transparent-background call-out badge plate — solid brand-color badge region, same selection-fill technique, for on-screen fact call-outs.

outputs.name: graded_endcard_frame.jpg
outputs.spec: End-card hero frame graded to match the locked brand look, with right-side negative space for the logo lockup.

outputs.name: northloop_asset_board
outputs.spec: Firefly board deep-link gathering the graded reference, plates, logo SVG, cleaned VO, and video deliverables as one organised linked-asset set for the Premiere handoff.

connector_workflow.note: Begin uploading the five client-supplied assets (raw clips bundle, scratch VO wav, logo png, brand color reference jpg, end-card hero jpg) into CC storage to start the chain.
Finalize the multipart uploads; returns the asset IDs/URIs for clips, VO, logo, color-reference, and end-card used by every downstream step.
Denoise/de-room the scratch voiceover (voice-only) so it can drive the cut. [A] async-widget.
Summarize the raw clips bundle to agree on key talking points / logical flow before cutting. Consumes the uploaded video asset. [A].
Build the ~60s highlight/sizzle cut from the raw clips, prompted by the agreed key points from step 4. [A] async-widget.
Reframe the sizzle cut to 9:16 vertical for social teasers — SAME length, no trimming. Consumes the step-5 cut. [A].
Straighten the deliberately-tilted brand color-reference still (consumes the uploaded color-reference). [C].
Crop the straightened reference clean to remove the loose headroom. Consumes step-7 output. [C].
Lay down an auto-tone baseline on the cropped reference before manual grading. Consumes step-8. [C].
Set overall exposure for the brand look. Consumes step-9. [C].
Roll off highlights for the soft film-like top end. Consumes step-10. [C].
Lift the dark portions for a lifted-shadow cinematic base. Consumes step-11. [C].
Push warm color temperature to establish the amber brand tone. Consumes step-12. [C].
Shape the amber/teal hue-sat-lum split that defines the look. Consumes step-13. [C].
Final vibrance/saturation balance on the reference grade. Consumes step-14. [C].
Apply a gentle Lightroom preset to seal the cinematic character. Consumes step-15. [C].
Add subtle film grain to finalize the authoritative locked brand-look reference frame. Consumes step-16. [C].
Vectorize the flat logo PNG to a clean scalable SVG for end-card and corner bug. Consumes the uploaded logo. [C].

On the vectorized logo's already-transparent canvas (reused as the plate canvas, NOT the opaque graded frame), select a horizontal lower-third bar region so the plate keeps a genuine transparent background. Consumes step-18. [C].
Solid-fill the selected region with brand amber (#E8A13A) to produce the lower-third bar plate (solid-color fill only). Consumes step-19. [C].
On the same plate canvas, select a rounded badge region for the call-out. Consumes step-20. [C].
Solid-fill the badge region with the deep-navy accent to produce the call-out badge plate. Consumes step-21. [C].
Begin grading the end-card hero frame: auto-tone baseline on the uploaded ungraded end-card still. [C].
Warm the end-card to MATCH the locked brand-look reference frame from step 17, finishing the graded end-card. [C].
Gather every produced deliverable (cleaned VO, both video cuts, graded reference, logo SVG, both plates, graded end-card) into one Firefly board deep-link as the organised linked-asset handoff. [C].

chaining_note: Strict output→input chain. Steps 1-2 load the five client assets into CC storage (uploaded_assets). Three sub-chains then fan out and feed a final packaging step: (AUDIO/VIDEO [A]) media_enhance_speech consumes the uploaded VO; media_summarize consumes the uploaded clips → its footage_summary prompts video_create_quick_cut (sizzle_cut_1080p) → which feeds video_resize (vertical reframe). (BRAND-LOOK [C]) a 11-step linear grade pipeline threads the color-reference still through every link — auto_straighten→crop_to_bounds→apply_auto_tone→adjust_exposure→adjust_highlights→adjust_dark_portions→adjust_color_temperature→adjust_hsl→adjust_vibrance_and_saturation→apply_preset→add_grain — each step consuming the prior step's frame, producing the locked brand_look_reference_frame. (PLATES [C]) image_vectorize consumes the uploaded logo to produce logo_vector.svg; the lower-third/call-out plates then use that vectorized logo's already-transparent canvas as the plate canvas (never the opaque graded frame), so both plates keep a genuine transparent background: select_by_prompt→fill_area (bar) → its output feeds select_by_prompt→fill_area (badge). The end-card grade consumes the uploaded hero still AND the locked reference frame as its match target (step 24). Finally create_firefly_board (step 25) consumes all eight deliverables. Nothing after step 2 starts from scratch; every step reads a prior step's output.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 25 connector calls, 21 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four groups (asset/storage, video/audio [A], photo/raster grade [C], vector + board). It exercises an 11-link linear color-grade dependency chain (each adjust_* consuming the previous frame), a video sub-chain where a summary prompts a sizzle cut that is then reframed, a selection→solid-fill plate sub-chain reused twice, and a cross-branch match (end-card grade references the locked look frame). Difficulty comes from (a) honoring the [A] async vs [C] headless split correctly — the video/audio deliverables are async-widget, the stills/plates fully headless; (b) respecting the hard limits (no compositing of text onto video, no gen-fill, fill_area solid-only, video_resize same-length) while still satisfying the brief's 'motion graphics / text overlays' intent via standalone graphic plates; and (c) keeping every post-load step strictly output→input chained into a single coherent Premiere-handoff package.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capabilities present. image_fill_area is used only for solid-color fill of a selected region within an existing (now transparent) canvas — it does not extend/square/widen the canvas. No generative-expand/outpaint, no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no compositing of text onto video, no video trim-to-timestamp, no format-convert. video_resize keeps the same length; media_enhance_speech is voice-only; image_apply_lens_blur is not used. All 21 distinct tools are real Adobe-connector actions with proper [A] async / [C] headless tagging.
reverify.notes: The only flagged issue (plate transparency not guaranteed because plates were solid-filled onto an opaque graded photo frame) is resolved by re-sourcing the plate sub-chain from the vectorized logo's already-transparent canvas. Step 19 inputs_from now points to logo_vector.svg; its note and the chaining_note are reconciled; the two plate output specs already read 'Transparent-background', now genuinely guaranteed. Task remains real, coherent, and fully connector-doable.
